claimText: 3I/ATLAS is an interstellar comet discovered by the NASA-funded ATLAS survey telescope located in Chile.
evidenceExcerpt: NASA funded ATLAS (Asteroid Terrestrial-impact Last Alert System) survey telescope in Chile discovered an interstellar Comet named 3I/ATLAS.
examUse: Statement-based question on recent space discoveries and the location of the ATLAS telescope.
distractorHooks: ATLAS telescope is located in Hawaii or India

claimText: 3I/ATLAS is categorized as an interstellar comet because of the hyperbolic shape of its orbital path.
evidenceExcerpt: It is an active comet categorized as interstellar because of the hyperbolic shape of its orbital path.
examUse: Conceptual question on the criteria for classifying celestial bodies as interstellar.
distractorHooks: Categorized as interstellar due to its chemical composition
Has an elliptical or parabolic orbital path

claimText: The interstellar comet 3I/ATLAS does not follow a closed orbital path around the Sun.
evidenceExcerpt: It does not follow a closed orbital path about the Sun.
examUse: Statement-based question on the orbital characteristics of interstellar comets.
distractorHooks: Follows a highly elliptical closed orbit around the Sun
Orbits the Earth instead of the Sun
